name: Peanut Butter & Jelly Filled Spiders
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare two baking sheets with cooking spray; set aside.
2. In a small bowl, combine peanut butter and jam; set aside.
3. Divide dough into sixteen equal pieces.
4. It takes two pieces of dough to make one spider.
5. To make the spider, take one piece of dough on a lightly floured surface, divide this piece into four equal pieces for the spider's legs.
6. Roll each of these pieces into thin ropes, each being eight inches long.
7. Lay each of these ropes, parallel and equal in length to each other, half inch apart onto prepared baking sheets.
8. For the spider's body, divide a second piece of dough (twice as large as the smaller piece), on a floured surface, pat larger piece into a circle with the palm of your hand.
9. Place two teaspoons of peanut butter and jam mixture onto the center of the circle.
10. Bring ends of dough up together and seal seams.
11. Gently roll this piece of dough into a ball; set aside.
12. Roll smaller piece of dough into a round ball (this will be the spider's head).
13. Place the peanut butter-filled body and head centered on top of the four ropes of dough.
14. The spider should now have eight legs.
15. Finish with remaining pieces of dough to make eight spiders.
16. Let rise until doubled in size, one hour.
17. Bake in a 375 degree oven for ten minutes, or until golden brown.
